PgGuide.title: Postgraduate Programme Guide
PgGuide.subtitle: An overview of postgraduate study in Computer Science at Stellenbosch — programmes, structure, and career pathways.
PgGuide.overviewText: The Department of Computer Science at Stellenbosch University offers three postgraduate programmes: Honours (BScHons), Masters (MSc), and PhD. Over the last forty-five years, more than 600 talented students have passed through our Honours programme alone.
PgGuide.overviewText2: Our postgraduate programmes develop advanced research skills, critical thinking, and deep technical expertise. Whether you want to pursue a career in industry or continue into academia, postgraduate study in CS at Stellenbosch is excellent preparation.
PgGuide.programmesSubtitle: Choose the level that aligns with your academic journey and career goals.
PgGuide.honoursDesc: An intensive one-year programme combining advanced coursework with an independent research project. Excellent preparation for industry or further study.
PgGuide.mastersDesc: A thesis-based programme focused on conducting world-class research under the guidance of an expert supervisor.
PgGuide.phdDesc: Doctoral research producing an original, cutting-edge contribution to the field of Computer Science.
PgGuide.step1Desc: Honours, Masters, or PhD — each has different requirements and structure.
PgGuide.step2Desc: Meet the entry requirements and submit your application before the deadline.
PgGuide.step3Desc: Finish coursework and/or research to earn your degree.
PgGuide.whyUsSubtitle: What makes our postgraduate programmes stand out.
PgGuide.strengthExpertDesc: Work alongside internationally recognised academics who are leaders in their fields.
PgGuide.strengthResearchDesc: Join one of 15+ research groups spanning AI, machine learning, data science, cybersecurity, and more.
PgGuide.strengthAIDesc: Our department is at the forefront of machine learning and artificial intelligence research in South Africa.
PgGuide.pathwayResearchDesc: Work at the cutting edge of technology in R&D roles at leading companies and research institutions worldwide.
PgGuide.pathwayIndustryDesc: Our graduates hold senior positions at top tech companies locally and internationally. A postgraduate degree can significantly impact your career trajectory.
PgGuide.pathwayAcademiaDesc: Continue into academic research and teaching, contributing to the next generation of computer scientists.
PgGuide.mlaiText: For information about the structured one-year MSc in Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence, including its specialised application procedure, visit the dedicated programme website.
PgGuide.modulesText: Browse all Honours, Masters, and PhD modules offered by the department, including advanced algorithms, AI, machine learning, data science, and software engineering.

PgModules.title: Postgraduate Modules
PgModules.subtitle: Modules offered across Honours, Masters, and PhD programmes in Computer Science.

PgProspective.title: Prospective Postgraduate Students
PgProspective.subtitle: Entry requirements, application procedures, and everything you need to know about joining our postgraduate programme.
PgProspective.introText: Postgraduate study in Computer Science at Stellenbosch offers the opportunity to develop advanced skills, conduct meaningful research, and prepare for leadership roles in industry or academia. Our programmes are designed around individual supervision, active research groups, and a supportive academic community.
PgProspective.entryReqIntro: Admission is based on selection criteria including academic performance and background alignment. Capacity is limited and admission is competitive — meeting minimum requirements does not guarantee acceptance.
honoursReqs: A degree that covers the CS curriculum
Average of at least 65% in final-year CS modules
At least 32 credits in quantitative modules at 2nd/3rd year level
PgProspective.mastersReqs: Honours degree in Computer Science or approved equivalent. Additional background may be required on a case-by-case basis.
PgProspective.phdReqs: A thesis-based Masters degree and the required Computer Science background.
PgProspective.prereqText: All honours modules require the four core third-year CS modules, Mathematics and Probability Theory & Statistics up to first year, and preferably at least one of Mathematics or Applied Mathematics up to second year.
howToApplySteps: For Masters/PhD: obtain agreement from a supervisor
Complete the application forms and upload required documentation
PgProspective.partTimeText: Part-time study is available. Honours can extend to two years; Masters to three-four years. We welcome international students — our modules are presented in English and are equivalent to 8 ECTS credits each.
PgProspective.fundingText: The university offers various bursaries and financial support. Teaching assistant positions can provide supplemental income. Some supervisors have research funds available for Masters/PhD students.
PgProspective.demiText: Most undergraduate modules require teaching assistants. While it won't fully cover your expenses, these positions provide supplemental income and are a wonderful way to consolidate your own knowledge.
PgProspective.questionsText: If you have specific questions about postgraduate study, please contact our postgraduate coordinator.

PgHonours.title: Honours in Computer Science
PgHonours.subtitle: A one-year full-time postgraduate programme that develops research skills and advanced CS knowledge.
PgHonours.overviewText: Over the last forty-five years, more than 600 talented students have passed through our programme. It is excellent preparation for pursuing either an MSc degree or a career in industry.
PgHonours.projectText: The honours project is worth 32 credits — a large software construction or research problem worked on independently under staff supervision throughout the year.
PgHonours.electivesText: Six elective modules of 16 credits each. Choose combinations focusing on Machine Learning, Data Science, Software Engineering, or a broader range.
PgHonours.maxExternal: At most two modules may be taken from related departments with permission.
highlights: Strong mix of theory and application courses
Independent research project with staff supervision
Graduates are highly sought after by industry
Can significantly impact your career trajectory

PgMasters.title: Masters in Computer Science
PgMasters.subtitle: A two-year thesis-based programme teaching world-class research skills.
PgMasters.overviewText: The goal is to teach students how to evaluate and conduct world-class research and become a master in their field. While the work is not required to be original cutting-edge research, it often is.
PgMasters.structureText: The Masters degree is based on thesis work under the guidance of a supervisor. You need the agreement of a staff member who will supervise your thesis.
howToStartSteps: Browse our Research pages to find areas of interest
Contact a potential supervisor to discuss topics
Obtain agreement from a supervisor before applying
If possible, arrange a face-to-face meeting
PgMasters.careerText: The reading, writing, and critical thinking skills acquired are highly sought after in industry. Most graduates who do not continue with a PhD go on to successful careers locally and abroad.

PgPhd.title: PhD in Computer Science
PgPhd.subtitle: Doctoral research producing original contributions to the field of Computer Science.
PgPhd.overviewText: A dissertation containing the results of your independent research is required. Our PhD programme offers the opportunity to work alongside internationally recognised researchers on cutting-edge problems.
PgPhd.requirementsText: A thesis-based Masters degree and the required Computer Science background. Additional requirements may be determined by your supervisor.
PgPhd.researchText: Our department has active research groups spanning artificial intelligence, machine learning, data science, cybersecurity, software engineering, and more.
PgPhd.supervisorText: Browse our Research pages and contact potential supervisors to discuss research topics. Obtaining a supervisor's agreement is essential before applying.

modules.name: Honours Project in Computer Science
modules.description: A large software construction or research problem on which the student works independently, under the supervision of a staff member.

modules.name: Advanced Algorithms
modules.description: This module continues from Computer Science 214 and covers advanced topics in the design and analysis of algorithms and associated data structures. Topics include a selection from algorithm design techniques, linear programming, approximation algorithms, randomised algorithms, probabilistic algorithms, parallel algorithms, number-theoretic algorithms, cryptanalysis, computational geometry, computational biology, network algorithms, and complexity theory.

modules.name: Adv. Topics I — Vulnerability Discovery and Exploitation
modules.description: This module provides an introduction to the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities in software systems. We learn to uncover vulnerabilities and demonstrate their exploitability and then proceed to focus on methods for automating parts of the discovery process, both via static and dynamic program analysis.

modules.name: Adv. Topics II — Ontology Engineering
modules.description: An overview of ontology engineering — including knowledge representation languages, automated reasoning, and methods and techniques for ontology development — and main application fields. Topics covered include logic foundations for ontologies, ontology development top-down and bottom-up, methodologies for ontology development and maintenance, methods to enhance ontology quality, and a selection of current research topics.

modules.name: Computing and Society
modules.description: Theory, domains and critique of topics related to Computing and Society, such as human-centred computing; social development theories, critical analysis of case studies; methods and ethics; and challenges of sustainable community engagement.

modules.name: Artificial Intelligence
modules.description: Introduction to a selection of topics in artificial intelligence and optimisation. Meta-heuristics. Swarm intelligence, including particle swarm optimisation and the ant colony meta-heuristic. Evolutionary computation, including genetic algorithms, genetic programming, evolutionary strategies, differential evolution, cultural algorithms, and coevolution. Hyper-heuristics. Fitness landscape analysis. Optimisation problems.

modules.name: Functional Programming
modules.description: This module gives an introduction to the functional programming paradigm.

modules.name: Machine Learning A
modules.description: Prominent machine-learning concepts and tasks. Selected feature extraction or dimensionality reduction techniques. Introduction to probabilistic modelling and latent variable models. Fundamental paradigms in parameter estimation.

modules.name: Computer Networks
modules.description: Introduction to networks in general and the internet in particular. Architecture and protocols. Allocation of resources and congestion control. Network security. Applications.

modules.name: Concurrent Programming I
modules.description: This module covers an overview of the field of concurrency, its theoretical principles, the design, implementation and verification of concurrent systems, and practical aspects of distributed and parallel programming. It is focused around selected important topics.

modules.name: Machine Learning
modules.description: This module is an introduction to selected topics in machine learning.

modules.name: Software Construction — Compilers
modules.description: An introduction to compiler design and construction, covering lexical analysis, parsing, semantic analysis, and code generation.

modules.name: Principles of Data Science
modules.description: This course covers the typical pipeline of data science projects: information retrieval, data wrangling and exploratory data analysis, hypothesis testing and regression analysis, as well as visualisations and data ethics.

modules.name: Space Science Algorithms
modules.description: Algorithms and techniques in Space Science, with applications.

modules.name: ML and AI I — Cognitive Robotics
modules.description: Topics covered are logic and knowledge representation, the Prolog language, deterministic automated planning, dynamic Bayesian networks and dynamic decision networks, Boolean and arithmetic circuits, the ProbLog language, probabilistic planning (based on Markov decision processes), and an introduction to reinforcement learning.

modules.name: Software Testing and Analysis
modules.description: Introduction to various techniques for software quality management.
modules.lecturer: Cornelia Inggs, Bernd Fischer, Willem Visser

modules.name: Automata Theory & Applications
modules.description: A first introduction to theoretical computer science, covering the Chomsky hierarchy of languages in relation to computability.
